title: alloc_subscription_address holds in-process mutex across DB calls to prevent diversifier reuse
description: ws.rs alloc_subscription_address() (~line 2073) and state.rs subscription_alloc_lock: the next_diversifier + find_address + advance_diversifier_to sequence is wrapped in a tokio Mutex to serialize concurrent SUBSCRIBE_REQUEST handlers. Without the mutex, two concurrent callers both read the same 'start' from next_diversifier (which is already incremented inside its own transaction), then both call find_address(start) and land on the same diversifier index, generating a duplicate Sapling address that maps to two different invoices. The mutex is the correct fix. A contributor might try to remove it thinking next_diversifier's own transaction is sufficient — it is not, because find_address() can skip ahead an unpredictable number of indices.
